pages.raw_text:
Water meters, expensive standpipes and big costly
reservoir.


That's what the experts sitting the water commission
have evolved for Spokane.


The commission turned in the report at o'clock this
afternoon to the mayor Tonight it will be read to the
city council.


Money galore will be needed if all the recommendations
OF the commission carried out It would look this
early in the movement that the city hall and the
half built library would have to be mortgaged


1t looks too the water Meter Gang would be able
to fight for the fosting of expensive meters
on the burdened taxpayers.


The details of the plans are of course not open to the
public until after they have been read to the council
tonight But enough is learned to warrant the statement
that it's going to be an expensive proposition.


The water commission has practically thrown away the
plans submitted by City Engineer Gill and Water Com
missioner Weymouth. In their stead plans pre-
pared by the commission experts.


These plans embrace 20 million gallon reservoir to be
located at what is known Stages orchard" near the
Southeast boulevard and and one half miles south of
Sprague avenue, west of the old Country club site.


In addition to this reservoir the commission recon-
mends expensive standppe which will about $1,000
year to


On top of all this burden the commission recommends
that the people be compelled to put in water meters


All this will be the result of the extraordinary sitting
of the experts who hired without stipulated salary
to give Spokane water system cheaper than that pro-
posed by Gill and Weymouth.
